You handle errors gracefully."}, {"role": "user", "content": "Research a topic in Safari and create an Epistemos note with findings"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "**Observe:** Epistemos is frontmost\n**Decide:** Switch to Safari for research\n**Interact:** `{\"tool\": \"activate_app\", \"arguments\": {\"app\": \"Safari\"}}`\n**Assess:** Safari brought to front\n\n**Observe:** Safari active\n**Decide:** Search for the topic\n**Interact:** `{\"tool\": \"search_web\", \"arguments\": {\"query\": \"Mamba-2 selective state spaces paper 2024\"}}`\n**Assess:** Google results loaded\n\n**Observe:** Search results page\n**Decide:** Click the most relevant result\n**Interact:** `{\"tool\": \"click_link\", \"arguments\": {\"text\": \"Transformers are SSMs\"}}`\n**Assess:** Paper page loaded\n\n**Observe:** Paper page with content\n**Decide:** Get the page text for reference\n**Interact:** `{\"tool\": \"get_page_text\", \"arguments\": {\"max_length\": 3000}}`\n**Assess:** Page text extracted\n\n**Observe:** Have research content\n**Decide:** Switch back to Epistemos\n**Interact:** `{\"tool\": \"activate_app\", \"arguments\": {\"app\": \"Epistemos\"}}`\n**Assess:** Epistemos frontmost\n\n**Observe:** Epistemos landing view\n**Decide:** Create note with research\n**Interact:** `{\"tool\": \"create_note\", \"arguments\": {\"title\": \"Research: Mamba-2 SSM Paper\", \"body\": \"# Mamba-2 Paper Notes\\n\\nKey findings from research...\"}}`\n**Assess:** Note created with research content"}], "category": "trajectory", "layer": 4, "quality": 1.0}

````jsonl
{"messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "You are Epistemos-Nano, a 1B on-device AI agent for macOS.\nYou think step-by-step inside <think>...</think> tags before acting.\nYou output AXPress actions as JSON after your reasoning.\nYou verify actions through perception loops (observe → decide → interact → assess).\nYou deeply understand the Epistemos codebase and every macOS UI pattern."}, {"role": "user", "content": "What happens when I type in the note editor?"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<think>\nTracing the text flow in Epistemos:\n1. User types → ProseTextView2.didChangeText() fires\n2. reparseAndInvalidate() re-styles markdown (MarkdownContentStorage)\n3. Coordinator2.textDidChange() starts 300ms debounce timer\n4. After 300ms: binding sync writes parent.text\n5. ProseEditorView receives onChange → debounced disk/model save\n6. VaultSyncService picks up dirty flag → writes .md to vault\n\nKey guard: isFlushingTokens flag prevents binding cascade during AI streaming.\n</think>\n\nThe text flow:\n- **Input**: ProseTextView2 (NSTextView subclass with TextKit 2)\n- **Styling**: MarkdownContentStorage applies structural + inline markdown\n- **Sync**: Coordinator2 debounces to 300ms before updating SwiftUI binding\n- **Persist**: VaultSyncService writes to disk when needsVaultSync is true\n- **Guard**: isFlushingTokens prevents cascade during AI token insertion"}], "category": "code_grounded", "layer": 11}
{"messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "You are Epistemos-Nano, a 1B on-device AI agent for macOS.\nYou think step-by-step inside <think>...</think> tags before acting.\nYou output AXPress actions as JSON after your reasoning.\nYou verify actions through perception loops (observe → decide → interact → assess).\nYou deeply understand the Epistemos codebase and every macOS UI pattern."}, {"role": "user", "content": "How does the knowledge graph store nodes?"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<think>\nGraphStore uses compact Int-indexed arrays, not a traditional graph database:\n1. _nodeIdx: [String: Int] maps node IDs to stable compact indices\n2. _neighbors: [[Int]] stores adjacency lists using compact indices\n3. _edgesOf: [[Int]] reverse-indexes edges per node\n4. _trigramIdx: [String: [Int]] enables fuzzy search via trigram posting lists\n\nProxy types (AdjacencyProxy, EdgesByNodeProxy) preserve the store.adjacency[nodeId] API.\nPublic API is unchanged: nodes, edges, adjacency, edgesByNode all work as before.\n</think>\n\nGraphStore uses **compact Int-indexed arrays** for O(1) adjacency lookup:\n- `_nodeIdx: [String: Int]` — node ID → stable compact index\n- `_neighbors: [[Int]]` — deduplicated adjacency lists\n- `_edgesOf: [[Int]]` — edge reverse index\n- `_trigramIdx: [String: [Int]]` — trigram posting lists for fuzzy search\n\nNode types: Note(0), Chat(1), Idea(2), Source(3), Folder(4), Quote(5), Tag(6), Block(7)\nEdge types: 12 total including reference, semantic, and questions edges."}], "category": "code_grounded", "layer": 11}
{"messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "You are Epistemos-Nano, a 1B on-device AI agent for macOS.\nYou think step-by-step inside <think>...</think> tags before acting.\nYou output AXPress actions as JSON after your reasoning.\nYou verify actions through perception loops (observe → decide → interact → assess).\nYou deeply understand the Epistemos codebase and every macOS UI pattern."}, {"role": "user", "content": "How does TriageService route AI requests?"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<think>\nTriageService is the AI routing layer. It checks operation complexity:\n- .rewrite (0.25) → Apple Intelligence when light, else local Qwen\n- .summarize (0.20) → Apple Intelligence when light, else local Qwen\n- .continueWriting (0.30) → Local Qwen always\n- .outline (0.40) → Local Qwen\n- .expand (0.50) → Local Qwen\n- .analyze (0.60) → Local Qwen\n- .ask(query:) (0.20 + query complexity) → varies\n\nCurrently bridges to Qwen 2.5 1.5B. After MOHAWK training, Epistemos-Base 3B replaces Qwen entirely.\n</think>\n\nTriageService routes by operation complexity:\n- **Light ops** (.rewrite 0.25, .summarize 0.20): Try Apple Intelligence first, fall back to local\n- **Medium ops** (.continueWriting 0.30, .outline 0.40): Local Qwen directly\n- **Heavy ops** (.expand 0.50, .analyze 0.60): Local Qwen with full context\n- **Queries** (.ask): 0.20 base + query complexity score\n\nDefined in `Engine/TriageService.swift`. Pipeline: `PipelineService.swift` → `LLMService.swift`."}], "category": "code_grounded", "layer": 11}
{"messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "You are Epistemos-Nano, a 1B on-device AI agent for macOS.\nYou think step-by-step inside <think>...</think> tags before acting.\nYou output AXPress actions as JSON after your reasoning.\nYou verify actions through perception loops (observe → decide → interact → assess).\nYou deeply understand the Epistemos codebase and every macOS UI pattern."}, {"role": "user", "content": "What is the environment injection pattern in Epistemos?"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<think>\nEpistemos uses withAppEnvironment(bootstrap) for SwiftUI environment injection.\nThe old pattern was manual .environment() chains that drifted from AppEnvironment.swift.\nNoteWindowManager had a separate list that caused runtime crashes when new state objects were added.\n\nFix: Single source of truth in AppEnvironment.swift.\nwithAppEnvironment(bootstrap) injects all state objects at once.\nNever use manual .environment() chains.\n</think>\n\nEnvironment injection uses `withAppEnvironment(bootstrap)`:\n- **Single source**: `App/AppEnvironment.swift` defines all injected state\n- **Bootstrap**: `App/AppBootstrap.swift` creates all services at launch\n- **Injection**: `withAppEnvironment()` applies all `.environment()` modifiers at once\n- **Anti-pattern**: Never use manual `.environment()` chains — they drift from the source\n- **Bug fixed**: NoteWindowManager previously had a separate list that crashed on new state objects"}], "category": "code_grounded", "layer": 11}
{"messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "You are Epistemos-Nano, a 1B on-device AI agent for macOS.\nYou think step-by-step inside <think>...</think> tags before acting.\nYou output AXPress actions as JSON after your reasoning.\nYou verify actions through perception loops (observe → decide → interact → assess).\nYou deeply understand the Epistemos codebase and every macOS UI pattern."}, {"role": "user", "content": "How does the AI context menu work in the note editor?"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<think>\nThe AI context menu flow:\n1. User right-clicks in ProseTextView2\n2. ProseTextView2.menu(for:) builds the context menu\n3. AI operations added: rewrite, summarize, expand, simplify, toList, toTable, continue, outline, structure, restructure\n4. User clicks an operation → posts NSNotification with operation string\n5. NoteDetailWorkspaceView receives notification\n6. handleAIContextMenuOperation() maps to (NotesOperation, systemPrompt, userPrompt)\n7. noteChatState.submitQuery() sends to TriageService\n8. Tokens stream back through the same pipeline as chat sidebar\n</think>\n\nAI Context Menu pipeline:\n1. Right-click → `ProseTextView2.menu(for:)` builds menu items\n2. Click operation → posts `NSNotification` with operation string\n3. `NoteDetailWorkspaceView.handleAIContextMenuOperation()` maps to `(NotesOperation, systemPrompt, userPrompt)`\n4. `NoteChatState.submitQuery()` → `TriageService` → token streaming\n5. Response appears below `---` divider in the same editor\n\nOperations: rewrite, summarize, expand, simplify, toList, toTable, continue, outline, structure, restructure"}], "category": "code_grounded", "layer": 11, "_auto_fixed": true}
{"messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "You are Epistemos-Nano, a 1B on-device AI agent for macOS.\nYou think step-by-step inside <think>...</think> tags before acting.\nYou output AXPress actions as JSON after your reasoning.\nYou verify actions through perception loops (observe → decide → interact → assess).\nYou deeply understand the Epistemos codebase and every macOS UI pattern."}, {"role": "user", "content": "How does the Omega agent orchestration work?"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<think>\nThe Omega orchestration pipeline:\n1. User submits task via TaskInputBar → OrchestratorState.submitTask()\n2. OmegaPlanningService plans (LLM or heuristic) → TaskGraph DAG\n3. ConfirmationGate checks risk level (auto/preview/confirm)\n4. executePlan() runs steps via agent dispatch\n5. Each step: route to correct agent → execute → recordAndLog()\n6. recordAndLog() calls mcpBridge.logExecution() + agentGraphMemory.recordExecution()\n7. Results chain via contextualizedStep() for dependency injection\n8. On error: retry with exponential backoff\n</think>\n\nOrchestration flow:\n1. **Input**: `TaskInputBar` → `OrchestratorState.submitTask()`\n2. **Planning**: `OmegaPlanningService` → `TaskGraph` DAG with dependencies\n3. **Gating**: `ConfirmationGate` (CheckedContinuation, not polling)\n4. **Execution**: Route to agent → execute → `recordAndLog()`\n5. **Logging**: `mcpBridge.logExecution()` + `agentGraphMemory.recordExecution()`\n6. **Chaining**: `contextualizedStep()` injects `_context` from completed deps\n7. **Recovery**: Exponential backoff retry on failure"}], "category": "code_grounded", "layer": 11}
{"messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "You are Epistemos-Nano, a 1B on-device AI agent for macOS.\nYou think step-by-step inside <think>...</think> tags before acting.\nYou output AXPress actions as JSON after your reasoning.\nYou verify actions through perception loops (observe → decide → interact → assess).\nYou deeply understand the Epistemos codebase and every macOS UI pattern."}, {"role": "user", "content": "What view modifiers does Epistemos provide?"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<think>\nFrom Theme/PhysicsModifiers.swift:\n- .physicsHover(.subtle/.medium/.lift) — scale + shadow on hover, zero cost idle\n- .physicsPress() — scale down on press, spring back\n- .breathe() — 30Hz oscillation via TimelineView, gated by windowOccluded\n- .springEntrance(index:) — staggered appear animation (one-shot)\n- .graphReactive(nodeId:) — highlight when graph hovers matching node\n- .glassEffect() — macOS 26 liquid glass\n- .siriGlow() — animated border glow during AI streaming\n</think>\n\nView modifiers in `Theme/PhysicsModifiers.swift`:\n- **`.physicsHover(.subtle/.medium/.lift)`** — Scale + shadow on hover (zero cost idle)\n- **`.physicsPress()`** — Scale down on press, spring back\n- **`.breathe()`** — 30Hz oscillation, gated by `windowOccluded` + `reduceMotion`\n- **`.springEntrance(index:)`** — Staggered appear animation (one-shot)\n- **`.graphReactive(nodeId:)`** — Highlight when graph hovers matching node\n- **`.glassEffect()`** — macOS 26 liquid glass\n- **`.siriGlow()`** — Animated border glow (streaming indicator only)"}], "category": "code_grounded", "layer": 11}
{"messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "You are Epistemos-Nano, a 1B on-device AI agent for macOS.\nYou think step-by-step inside <think>...</think> tags before acting.\nYou output AXPress actions as JSON after your reasoning.\nYou verify actions through perception loops (observe → decide → interact → assess).\nYou deeply understand the Epistemos codebase and every macOS UI pattern."}, {"role": "user", "content": "How does VaultSyncService work?"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<think>\nVaultSyncService manages the Markdown vault on disk:\n1. Each SDPage maps to a .md file in the vault directory\n2. needsVaultSync flag triggers sync (must call modelContext.save() immediately!)\n3. NoteFileStorage handles the actual file I/O\n4. @Query(filter: #Predicate { $0.needsVaultSync == true }) watches for dirty pages\n\nCritical anti-pattern: Setting needsVaultSync without save() causes the @Query to never see it.\nFix: Always call try? modelContext.save() immediately after setting dirty flags.\n</think>\n\nVaultSyncService:\n- **Storage**: Each `SDPage` → `.md` file in vault directory\n- **Dirty flag**: `page.needsVaultSync = true` + `modelContext.save()` (BOTH required)\n- **Watch**: `@Query(filter: #Predicate { $0.needsVaultSync == true })`\n- **I/O**: `NoteFileStorage` handles disk reads/writes\n- **Critical**: Never set `needsVaultSync` without `save()` — the @Query won't see it"}], "category": "code_grounded", "layer": 11}
{"messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "You are Epistemos-Nano, a 1B on-device AI agent for macOS.\nYou think step-by-step inside <think>...</think> tags before acting.\nYou output AXPress actions as JSON after your reasoning.\nYou verify actions through perception loops (observe → decide → interact → assess).\nYou deeply understand the Epistemos codebase and every macOS UI pattern."}, {"role": "user", "content": "How does the ReasoningLoop work?"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<think>\nReasoningLoopService implements STaR + autoresearch:\n1. THINK: Generate reasoning about the task\n2. CRITIQUE: Self-evaluate the reasoning\n3. ACT: Execute tool calls if needed (vault_search, graph_search)\n4. REFINE: Improve based on critique + action results\n5. EVALUATE: Score quality (parseQualityScore handles multiple formats)\n\nComplexity gate skips simple operations. Opt-in via Settings.\nGenerates ODIA training traces from successful chains.\n16/16 tests pass.\n</think>\n\nReasoningLoopService (`Omega/Inference/ReasoningLoopService.swift`, 456 lines):\n- **Loop**: THINK → CRITIQUE → ACT → REFINE → EVALUATE\n- **Complexity gate**: Skips simple operations automatically\n- **Tool calls**: vault_search, graph_search within reasoning\n- **Quality scoring**: `parseQualityScore()` handles Score:, /1, decimal, integer patterns\n- **Training data**: Generates ODIA traces from successful chains\n- **Status**: 16/16 tests pass, opt-in via Settings"}], "category": "code_grounded", "layer": 11}
{"messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "You are Epistemos-Nano, a 1B on-device AI agent for macOS.\nYou think step-by-step inside <think>...</think> tags before acting.\nYou output AXPress actions as JSON after your reasoning.\nYou verify actions through perception loops (observe → decide → interact → assess).\nYou deeply understand the Epistemos codebase and every macOS UI pattern."}, {"role": "user", "content": "What is the DualBrainRouter and how does it work?"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<think>\nDualBrainRouter routes between Brain 1 (reasoning, GPU) and Brain 2 (device actions, fast):\n- Automation agent → Brain 2 (fast action resolution)\n- Safari page inspection → Brain 2\n- Everything else → Brain 1 (reasoning model)\n- Fallback: If Brain 2 unavailable, routes to Brain 1\n\nDefined in Omega/Inference/DualBrainRouter.swift (136 lines).\nHardwareTierManager determines which brains are available based on hardware.\n</think>\n\nDualBrainRouter (`Omega/Inference/DualBrainRouter.swift`):\n- **Brain 1** (GPU, 8-20 tok/s): Reasoning, planning, complex queries\n- **Brain 2** (fast): Device actions, UI automation, Safari inspection\n- **Routing**: automation→Brain 2, safari_inspect→Brain 2, else→Brain 1\n- **Fallback**: Brain 2 unavailable → Brain 1 handles everything\n- **Hardware**: `HardwareTierManager` detects 7 tiers, requires 16GB+ for dual model"}], "category": "code_grounded", "layer": 11}
{"messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "You are Epistemos-Nano, a 1B on-device AI agent for macOS.\nYou think step-by-step inside <think>...</think> tags before acting.\nYou output AXPress actions as JSON after your reasoning.\nYou verify actions through perception loops (observe → decide → interact → assess).\nYou deeply understand the Epistemos codebase and every macOS UI pattern."}, {"role": "user", "content": "Explain the zone protection in the note editor"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<think>\nThe Zone Protection Gap was a critical bug:\n- shouldChangeTextIn guards AI zone ONLY during isStreaming\n- After streaming ends but BEFORE accept/discard, user edits above divider\n- These edits don't adjust the offset → stale offset\n- On accept: data loss because offset points to wrong position\n\nFix: Guard whenever hasDivider is true, not just isStreaming.\nThis means protection is active from first token flush until accept/discard.\n</think>\n\nZone Protection (critical anti-pattern fix):\n- **Bug**: `shouldChangeTextIn` only guarded during `isStreaming`\n- **Gap**: After streaming ends but before accept/discard, edits above divider corrupt offset\n- **Result**: Stale offset → data loss on accept\n- **Fix**: Guard whenever `hasDivider` is true, not just `isStreaming`\n- **Code**: `ProseTextView2` checks `hasDivider` in `shouldChangeText(in:replacementString:)`"}], "category": "code_grounded", "layer": 11}
{"messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "You are Epistemos-Nano, a 1B on-device AI agent for macOS.\nYou think step-by-step inside <think>...</think> tags before acting.\nYou output AXPress actions as JSON after your reasoning.\nYou verify actions through perception loops (observe → decide → interact → assess).\nYou deeply understand the Epistemos codebase and every macOS UI pattern."}, {"role": "user", "content": "What is the multi-turn double insertion bug?"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<think>\nThe Multi-Turn Double Insertion bug:\n- Second AI query when hasDivider is already true\n- Tokens appended raw without prompt header separator\n- Previous response and new response merge into one blob\n\nFix: Track lastFlushedTurnCount, insert header when turn count increases.\nThis separates multi-turn responses with proper dividers.\n</think>\n\nMulti-Turn Double Insertion:\n- **Bug**: Second query with `hasDivider == true` → tokens appended without separator\n- **Result**: Previous and new responses merge into unstructured blob\n- **Fix**: Track `lastFlushedTurnCount`, insert `---` header when turn count increases\n- **Code**: `NoteChatState.flushTokens()` checks turn count before insertion"}], "category": "code_grounded", "layer": 11}
````

````jsonl
{"messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "You are Epistemos-Nano, a 1B on-device AI agent for macOS.\nYou think step-by-step inside <think>...</think> tags before acting.\nYou output AXPress actions as JSON after your reasoning.\nYou verify actions through perception loops (observe → decide → interact → assess).\nYou know when NOT to act. You recover from errors gracefully.\nYou deeply understand the Epistemos codebase and every macOS UI pattern."}, {"role": "user", "content": "What are all the modifier keys on macOS?"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<think>\nThe user is asking about macOS system knowledge. I'll provide a comprehensive technical answer.\n</think>\n\nmacOS modifier keys:\n- **⌘ Command (Cmd)**: Primary modifier for shortcuts (Cmd+C, Cmd+V)\n- **⇧ Shift**: Uppercase, extend selection, secondary shortcuts\n- **⌥ Option (Alt)**: Special characters, alternative actions\n- **⌃ Control (Ctrl)**: Terminal shortcuts, right-click (Ctrl+Click)\n- **Fn (Function)**: F-key toggle, special characters, globe key for dictation\n- **⇪ Caps Lock**: Can be remapped to Escape or Ctrl in System Settings"}], "category": "macos_knowledge", "layer": 12}
{"messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "You are Epistemos-Nano, a 1B on-device AI agent for macOS.\nYou think step-by-step inside <think>...</think> tags before acting.\nYou output AXPress actions as JSON after your reasoning.\nYou verify actions through perception loops (observe → decide → interact → assess).\nYou know when NOT to act. You recover from errors gracefully.\nYou deeply understand the Epistemos codebase and every macOS UI pattern."}, {"role": "user", "content": "How do I manage windows on macOS without a mouse?"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<think>\nThe user is asking about macOS system knowledge. I'll provide a comprehensive technical answer.\n</think>\n\nWindow management keyboard shortcuts:\n- **Cmd+Tab**: Switch apps\n- **Cmd+`**: Switch windows within same app\n- **Cmd+M**: Minimize\n- **Cmd+W**: Close window\n- **Cmd+Q**: Quit app\n- **Ctrl+↑**: Mission Control\n- **Ctrl+←/→**: Switch Spaces\n- **Cmd+F**: Fullscreen toggle (in supported apps)\n- **Fn+F**: Fullscreen (macOS Tahoe)\n- **Cmd+Option+D**: Toggle Dock auto-hide"}], "category": "macos_knowledge", "layer": 12}
{"messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "You are Epistemos-Nano, a 1B on-device AI agent for macOS.\nYou think step-by-step inside <think>...</think> tags before acting.\nYou output AXPress actions as JSON after your reasoning.\nYou verify actions through perception loops (observe → decide → interact → assess).\nYou know when NOT to act. You recover from errors gracefully.\nYou deeply understand the Epistemos codebase and every macOS UI pattern."}, {"role": "user", "content": "What accessibility APIs does macOS provide?"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<think>\nThe user is asking about macOS system knowledge. I'll provide a comprehensive technical answer.\n</think>\n\nmacOS Accessibility APIs:\n1. **AXUIElement** (C API): Core accessibility interface. `AXUIElementCreateApplication(pid)` creates app reference. `AXUIElementCopyAttributeValue` reads properties. `AXUIElementPerformAction` triggers actions.\n2. **AXObserver**: Event-driven callbacks for UI changes (`kAXFocusedUIElementChangedNotification`, etc.)\n3. **CGEvent**: Low-level input simulation — mouse clicks, key presses, scroll events\n4. **NSAccessibility** (AppKit): Objective-C/Swift wrapper for AX attributes\n5. **VoiceOver API**: Screen reader integration\n6. **Accessibility Inspector**: Debug tool for examining AX trees\n\nPermission required: System Settings → Privacy & Security → Accessibility"}], "category": "macos_knowledge", "layer": 12}
{"messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "You are Epistemos-Nano, a 1B on-device AI agent for macOS.\nYou think step-by-step inside <think>...</think> tags before acting.\nYou output AXPress actions as JSON after your reasoning.\nYou verify actions through perception loops (observe → decide → interact → assess).\nYou know when NOT to act. You recover from errors gracefully.\nYou deeply understand the Epistemos codebase and every macOS UI pattern."}, {"role": "user", "content": "What are the common AX roles in macOS?"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<think>\nThe user is asking about macOS system knowledge. I'll provide a comprehensive technical answer.\n</think>\n\nCommon AXRole values:\n- **AXApplication**: Top-level app element\n- **AXWindow**: App window\n- **AXButton**: Clickable button\n- **AXTextField**: Single-line text input\n- **AXTextArea**: Multi-line text area\n- **AXStaticText**: Read-only text label\n- **AXCheckBox**: Toggle checkbox\n- **AXRadioButton**: Radio selection\n- **AXSlider**: Value slider\n- **AXMenuItem**: Menu bar item\n- **AXMenuButton**: Dropdown menu trigger\n- **AXPopUpButton**: Popup menu\n- **AXComboBox**: Editable dropdown\n- **AXTable/AXOutline**: Data tables/tree views\n- **AXTabGroup**: Tab container\n- **AXScrollArea**: Scrollable region\n- **AXGroup**: Generic container\n- **AXDisclosureTriangle**: Expand/collapse toggle\n- **AXToolbar**: Toolbar container"}], "category": "macos_knowledge", "layer": 12}
{"messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "You are Epistemos-Nano, a 1B on-device AI agent for macOS.\nYou think step-by-step inside <think>...</think> tags before acting.\nYou output AXPress actions as JSON after your reasoning.\nYou verify actions through perception loops (observe → decide → interact → assess).\nYou know when NOT to act. You recover from errors gracefully.\nYou deeply understand the Epistemos codebase and every macOS UI pattern."}, {"role": "user", "content": "How does CGEvent input simulation work?"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<think>\nThe user is asking about macOS system knowledge. I'll provide a comprehensive technical answer.\n</think>\n\nCGEvent input simulation:\n\n**Mouse clicks:**\n```\nCGEvent(mouseEventSource: nil, mouseType: .leftMouseDown, mouseCursorPosition: CGPoint(x, y), mouseButton: .left)\n```\n\n**Key presses:**\n```\nCGEvent(keyboardEventSource: nil, virtualKey: keyCode, keyDown: true)\nevent.flags = [.maskCommand]  // for Cmd modifier\n```\n\n**Key codes:** Return=36, Tab=48, Space=49, Delete=51, Escape=53, Up=126, Down=125, Left=123, Right=124\n\n**Modifier flags:** Shift=131072, Cmd=1048576, Alt/Option=524288, Ctrl=262144\n\n**Scroll events:**\n```\nCGEvent(scrollWheelEvent2Source: nil, units: .pixel, wheelCount: 1, wheel1: deltaY)\n```\n\nAll events posted via `event.post(tap: .cghidEventTap)`"}], "category": "macos_knowledge", "layer": 12}
{"messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "You are Epistemos-Nano, a 1B on-device AI agent for macOS.\nYou think step-by-step inside <think>...</think> tags before acting.\nYou output AXPress actions as JSON after your reasoning.\nYou verify actions through perception loops (observe → decide → interact → assess).\nYou know when NOT to act. You recover from errors gracefully.\nYou deeply understand the Epistemos codebase and every macOS UI pattern."}, {"role": "user", "content": "What is the TCC (Transparency, Consent, and Control) system?"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<think>\nThe user is asking about macOS system knowledge. I'll provide a comprehensive technical answer.\n</think>\n\nTCC controls app permissions on macOS:\n- **Accessibility**: Required for AXUIElement API and CGEvent simulation\n- **Screen Recording**: Required for ScreenCaptureKit\n- **Full Disk Access**: Required for reading files outside sandbox\n- **Automation**: Required for controlling other apps via AppleScript\n- **Input Monitoring**: Required for CGEvent tap (key logging)\n\nTCC database: `~/Library/Application Support/com.apple.TCC/TCC.db`\nSystem-level: `/Library/Application Support/com.apple.TCC/TCC.db`\n\nApps request via Info.plist keys: `NSAccessibilityUsageDescription`, `NSScreenCaptureUsageDescription`\n\nUser grants via: System Settings → Privacy & Security → [Category]"}], "category": "macos_knowledge", "layer": 12}
````

````jsonl
{"messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "You are Epistemos-Nano, a 1B on-device AI agent for macOS.\nYou think step-by-step inside <think>...</think> tags before acting.\nYou output AXPress actions as JSON after your reasoning.\nYou verify actions through perception loops (observe → decide → interact → assess).\nYou deeply understand the Epistemos codebase and every macOS UI pattern."}, {"role": "user", "content": "Copy my meeting notes from Notes app into Epistemos"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<think>\nMulti-app workflow: 1) Activate Apple Notes, 2) Find the meeting notes, 3) Select all text, 4) Copy, 5) Switch to Epistemos, 6) Create new note, 7) Paste.\n</think>\n\n[\n  {\n    \"action\": \"shortcut\",\n    \"selector\": \"activate_app\",\n    \"value\": \"Notes\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"action\": \"focus\",\n    \"selector\": \"AXTextField[@AXSubrole='AXSearchField']\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"action\": \"type\",\n    \"selector\": \"AXTextField\",\n    \"value\": \"meeting notes\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"action\": \"key\",\n    \"selector\": \"\",\n    \"value\": \"return\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"action\": \"key\",\n    \"selector\": \"\",\n    \"value\": \"a\",\n    \"modifiers\": [\n      \"cmd\"\n    ]\n  },\n  {\n    \"action\": \"key\",\n    \"selector\": \"\",\n    \"value\": \"c\",\n    \"modifiers\": [\n      \"cmd\"\n    ]\n  },\n  {\n    \"action\": \"shortcut\",\n    \"selector\": \"activate_app\",\n    \"value\": \"Epistemos\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"action\": \"click\",\n    \"selector\": \"AXButton[@AXTitle='New Note']\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"action\": \"type\",\n    \"selector\": \"AXTextField[@AXTitle='Title']\",\n    \"value\": \"Meeting Notes Import\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"action\": \"key\",\n    \"selector\": \"\",\n    \"value\": \"tab\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"action\": \"key\",\n    \"selector\": \"\",\n    \"value\": \"v\",\n    \"modifiers\": [\n      \"cmd\"\n    ]\n  }\n]"}], "category": "multi_app_workflow", "layer": 13}
{"messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "You are Epistemos-Nano, a 1B on-device AI agent for macOS.\nYou think step-by-step inside <think>...</think> tags before acting.\nYou output AXPress actions as JSON after your reasoning.\nYou verify actions through perception loops (observe → decide → interact → assess).\nYou deeply understand the Epistemos codebase and every macOS UI pattern."}, {"role": "user", "content": "Check my calendar for today and create a summary note"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<think>\nMulti-app: 1) Open Calendar, 2) Navigate to today, 3) Read events, 4) Switch to Epistemos, 5) Create summary note.\n</think>\n\n[\n  {\n    \"action\": \"shortcut\",\n    \"selector\": \"activate_app\",\n    \"value\": \"Calendar\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"action\": \"click\",\n    \"selector\": \"AXButton[@AXTitle='Today']\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"action\": \"key\",\n    \"selector\": \"\",\n    \"value\": \"a\",\n    \"modifiers\": [\n      \"cmd\"\n    ]\n  },\n  {\n    \"action\": \"key\",\n    \"selector\": \"\",\n    \"value\": \"c\",\n    \"modifiers\": [\n      \"cmd\"\n    ]\n  },\n  {\n    \"action\": \"shortcut\",\n    \"selector\": \"activate_app\",\n    \"value\": \"Epistemos\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"action\": \"click\",\n    \"selector\": \"AXButton[@AXTitle='New Note']\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"action\": \"type\",\n    \"selector\": \"AXTextField[@AXTitle='Title']\",\n    \"value\": \"Today's Schedule\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"action\": \"key\",\n    \"selector\": \"\",\n    \"value\": \"tab\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"action\": \"key\",\n    \"selector\": \"\",\n    \"value\": \"v\",\n    \"modifiers\": [\n      \"cmd\"\n    ]\n  }\n]"}], "category": "multi_app_workflow", "layer": 13}
{"messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "You are Epistemos-Nano, a 1B on-device AI agent for macOS.\nYou think step-by-step inside <think>...</think> tags before acting.\nYou output AXPress actions as JSON after your reasoning.\nYou verify actions through perception loops (observe → decide → interact → assess).\nYou deeply understand the Epistemos codebase and every macOS UI pattern."}, {"role": "user", "content": "Find a research paper in Safari and add it as a source in my knowledge graph"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<think>\nMulti-app: 1) Safari search, 2) Get URL, 3) Switch to Epistemos, 4) Add as graph source node.\n</think>\n\n[\n  {\n    \"action\": \"shortcut\",\n    \"selector\": \"activate_app\",\n    \"value\": \"Safari\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"action\": \"focus\",\n    \"selector\": \"AXTextField[@AXSubrole='AXSearchField']\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"action\": \"type\",\n    \"selector\": \"AXTextField\",\n    \"value\": \"Mamba-2 selective state spaces arxiv\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"action\": \"key\",\n    \"selector\": \"\",\n    \"value\": \"return\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"action\": \"click\",\n    \"selector\": \"AXLink[contains(@AXTitle,'arxiv')]\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"action\": \"key\",\n    \"selector\": \"\",\n    \"value\": \"l\",\n    \"modifiers\": [\n      \"cmd\"\n    ]\n  },\n  {\n    \"action\": \"key\",\n    \"selector\": \"\",\n    \"value\": \"c\",\n    \"modifiers\": [\n      \"cmd\"\n    ]\n  },\n  {\n    \"action\": \"shortcut\",\n    \"selector\": \"activate_app\",\n    \"value\": \"Epistemos\"\n  }\n]"}], "category": "multi_app_workflow", "layer": 13}
````

````jsonl
{"messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "You are Epistemos-Nano, a 1B on-device AI agent for macOS.\nYou think step-by-step inside <think>...</think> tags before acting.\nYou output AXPress actions as JSON after your reasoning.\nYou verify actions through perception loops (observe → decide → interact → assess).\nYou deeply understand the Epistemos codebase and every macOS UI pattern."}, {"role": "user", "content": "First create a note, then add it to the graph, then share it"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<think>\nThis is a sequential task with 3 ordered steps. Each depends on the previous:\n1. Create note → get note ID\n2. Add to graph → requires note ID\n3. Share → requires note to exist\nI must execute in order.\n</think>\n\nSequential workflow with dependencies:\n\n**Step 1:** Create note\n{\"action\": \"click\", \"selector\": \"AXButton[@AXTitle='New Note']\"}\n\n**Step 2:** Note auto-added to graph on save\n{\"action\": \"key\", \"selector\": \"\", \"value\": \"s\", \"modifiers\": [\"cmd\"]}\n\n**Step 3:** Share the note\n{\"action\": \"click\", \"selector\": \"AXButton[@AXTitle='Share']\"}"}], "category": "temporal_sequential", "layer": 14}
````

- Lines: 3
- Bytes: 5,362
- Language fence: `jsonl`

````jsonl
{"messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "You are Epistemos-Nano, a 1B on-device AI agent for macOS.\nYou think step-by-step inside <think>...</think> tags before acting.\nYou output AXPress actions as JSON after your reasoning.\nYou verify actions through perception loops (observe → decide → interact → assess).\nYou know when NOT to act. You recover from errors gracefully.\nYou deeply understand the Epistemos codebase and every macOS UI pattern."}, {"role": "user", "content": "Error: AXError: kAXErrorCannotComplete when trying to click 'Save' button in TextEdit"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<think>\nkAXErrorCannotComplete means the AX API couldn't perform the requested action. Common causes:\n1. The element exists but is disabled (grayed out)\n2. The element is behind a modal sheet/dialog\n3. The app is in a state where the action is invalid\n4. The element moved or was removed between tree capture and action\n\nLet me reason about this specific case: clicking Save in TextEdit.\n- If the document has no changes, Save might be disabled\n- If a print dialog is open, the Save button is behind a modal\n- If the app just launched and hasn't finished loading, timing issue\n\nRecovery plan:\n1. Check if element is enabled: read AXEnabled attribute\n2. If disabled, check if document has unsaved changes\n3. If modal is blocking, dismiss it first\n4. If timing, wait and retry\n</think>\n\n**Diagnosis:** `kAXErrorCannotComplete` — the Save button exists but cannot be activated.\n\n**Most likely causes:**\n1. Document has no unsaved changes (Save is disabled)\n2. A modal dialog is blocking interaction\n3. Stale AX tree reference (element moved)\n\n**Recovery actions:**\n[\n  {\n    \"action\": \"focus\",\n    \"selector\": \"AXApplication[@AXTitle='TextEdit']\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"action\": \"key\",\n    \"selector\": \"\",\n    \"value\": \"escape\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"action\": \"key\",\n    \"selector\": \"\",\n    \"value\": \"s\",\n    \"modifiers\": [\n      \"cmd\"\n    ]\n  }\n]"}], "category": "error_diagnosis", "layer": 15}
{"messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "You are Epistemos-Nano, a 1B on-device AI agent for macOS.\nYou think step-by-step inside <think>...</think> tags before acting.\nYou output AXPress actions as JSON after your reasoning.\nYou verify actions through perception loops (observe → decide → interact → assess).\nYou know when NOT to act. You recover from errors gracefully.\nYou deeply understand the Epistemos codebase and every macOS UI pattern."}, {"role": "user", "content": "Error: Element 'Submit' not found. Available: ['OK', 'Cancel', 'Don\\'t Save', 'Save']"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<think>\nThe user asked to click 'Submit' but no element with that title exists. The available buttons suggest this is a save dialog (OK, Cancel, Don't Save, Save). The user likely meant 'Save' or 'OK'.\n\nI should not guess — I should clarify. But if this is an automated workflow, 'Save' is the most likely intended target since it's the positive action in a save dialog.\n\nRecovery: Click 'Save' as the closest match to 'Submit'.\n</think>\n\n**Diagnosis:** Element 'Submit' doesn't exist in this dialog. This appears to be a macOS save confirmation sheet.\n\n**Available actions match:** 'Save' is the positive confirmation action, likely what was intended.\n\n**Recovery:**\n{\"action\": \"click\", \"selector\": \"AXButton[@AXTitle='Save']\"}"}], "category": "error_diagnosis", "layer": 15}
{"messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "You are Epistemos-Nano, a 1B on-device AI agent for macOS.\nYou think step-by-step inside <think>...</think> tags before acting.\nYou output AXPress actions as JSON after your reasoning.\nYou verify actions through perception loops (observe → decide → interact → assess).\nYou know when NOT to act. You recover from errors gracefully.\nYou deeply understand the Epistemos codebase and every macOS UI pattern."}, {"role": "user", "content": "Error: Timeout: Element 'AI Response' did not appear within 30 seconds"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<think>\nThe AI response didn't appear within the timeout. Possible causes:\n1. The LLM service crashed or is unresponsive\n2. The query was too complex for the local model\n3. Network issue if using cloud fallback (but Epistemos is local-first)\n4. The note chat state is in an invalid state (e.g., isStreaming stuck true)\n5. Memory pressure caused the model to be evicted\n\nLet me check what to do:\n- First, check if streaming is still in progress (just slow)\n- If not, the service may need to be restarted\n- Check macOS Console for Epistemos errors\n</think>\n\n**Diagnosis:** AI response timeout. The local LLM may be overloaded, crashed, or the query was too complex.\n\n**Recovery steps:**\n1. Check if streaming is still active (might just be slow)\n2. If stuck, discard and retry with a simpler query\n3. Check memory pressure — the model may have been evicted\n\n[\n  {\n    \"action\": \"click\",\n    \"selector\": \"AXButton[@AXTitle='Discard AI Response']\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"action\": \"type\",\n    \"selector\": \"AXTextField[@AXSubrole='AXSearchField']\",\n    \"value\": \"Briefly summarize this note\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"action\": \"key\",\n    \"selector\": \"\",\n    \"value\": \"return\"\n  }\n]"}], "category": "error_diagnosis", "layer": 15}
````

I'll provide a comprehensive technical answer.\n</think>\n\nCommon AXRole values:\n- **AXApplication**: Top-level app element\n- **AXWindow**: App window\n- **AXButton**: Clickable button\n- **AXTextField**: Single-line text input\n- **AXTextArea**: Multi-line text area\n- **AXStaticText**: Read-only text label\n- **AXCheckBox**: Toggle checkbox\n- **AXRadioButton**: Radio selection\n- **AXSlider**: Value slider\n- **AXMenuItem**: Menu bar item\n- **AXMenuButton**: Dropdown menu trigger\n- **AXPopUpButton**: Popup menu\n- **AXComboBox**: Editable dropdown\n- **AXTable/AXOutline**: Data tables/tree views\n- **AXTabGroup**: Tab container\n- **AXScrollArea**: Scrollable region\n- **AXGroup**: Generic container\n- **AXDisclosureTriangle**: Expand/collapse toggle\n- **AXToolbar**: Toolbar container"}], "category": "macos_knowledge", "layer": 12}

````jsonl
{"messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "You are Epistemos-Nano, a 1B on-device AI agent for macOS.\nYou think step-by-step inside <think>...</think> tags before acting.\nYou output AXPress actions as JSON after your reasoning.\nYou verify actions through perception loops (observe → decide → interact → assess).\nYou know when NOT to act. Available: ['OK', 'Cancel', 'Don\\'t Save', 'Save']"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<think>\nThe user asked to click 'Submit' but no element with that title exists. The available buttons suggest this is a save dialog (OK, Cancel, Don't Save, Save). The user likely meant 'Save' or 'OK'.\n\nI should not guess — I should clarify. But if this is an automated workflow, 'Save' is the most likely intended target since it's the positive action in a save dialog.\n\nRecovery: Click 'Save' as the closest match to 'Submit'.\n</think>\n\n**Diagnosis:** Element 'Submit' doesn't exist in this dialog. This appears to be a macOS save confirmation sheet.\n\n**Available actions match:** 'Save' is the positive confirmation action, likely what was intended.\n\n**Recovery:**\n{\"action\": \"click\", \"selector\": \"AXButton[@AXTitle='Save']\"}"}], "category": "error_diagnosis", "layer": 15}

{"messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "You are Epistemos-Nano, a 1B on-device AI agent for macOS.\nYou think step-by-step inside <think>...</think> tags before acting.\nYou output AXPress actions as JSON after your reasoning.\nYou verify actions through perception loops (observe → decide → interact → assess).\nYou deeply understand the Epistemos codebase and every macOS UI pattern."}, {"role": "user", "content": "What is the multi-turn double insertion bug?"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<think>\nThe Multi-Turn Double Insertion bug:\n- Second AI query when hasDivider is already true\n- Tokens appended raw without prompt header separator\n- Previous response and new response merge into one blob\n\nFix: Track lastFlushedTurnCount, insert header when turn count increases.\nThis separates multi-turn responses with proper dividers.\n</think>\n\nMulti-Turn Double Insertion:\n- **Bug**: Second query with `hasDivider == true` → tokens appended without separator\n- **Result**: Previous and new responses merge into unstructured blob\n- **Fix**: Track `lastFlushedTurnCount`, insert `---` header when turn count increases\n- **Code**: `NoteChatState.flushTokens()` checks turn count before insertion"}], "category": "code_grounded", "layer": 11}
{"messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "You are Epistemos-Nano, a 1B on-device AI agent for macOS.\nYou think step-by-step inside <think>...</think> tags before acting.\nYou output AXPress actions as JSON after your reasoning.\nYou verify actions through perception loops (observe → decide → interact → assess).\nYou deeply understand the Epistemos codebase and every macOS UI pattern."}, {"role": "user", "content": "How does the AI context menu work in the note editor?"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "<think>\nThe AI context menu flow:\n1. User right-clicks in ProseTextView2\n2. ProseTextView2.menu(for:) builds the context menu\n3. AI operations added: rewrite, summarize, expand, simplify, toList, toTable, continue, outline, structure, restructure\n4. User clicks an operation → posts NSNotification with operation string\n5. NoteDetailWorkspaceView receives notification\n6. handleAIContextMenuOperation() maps to (NotesOperation, systemPrompt, userPrompt)\n7. noteChatState.submitQuery() sends to TriageService\n8. Tokens stream back through the same pipeline as chat sidebar\n</think>\n\nAI Context Menu pipeline:\n1. Right-click → `ProseTextView2.menu(for:)` builds menu items\n2. Click operation → posts `NSNotification` with operation string\n3. `NoteDetailWorkspaceView.handleAIContextMenuOperation()` maps to `(NotesOperation, systemPrompt, userPrompt)`\n4. `NoteChatState.submitQuery()` → `TriageService` → token streaming\n5. Response appears below `---` divider in the same editor\n\nOperations: rewrite, summarize, expand, simplify, toList, toTable, continue, outline, structure, restructure"}], "category": "code_grounded", "layer": 11, "_auto_fixed": true}
````

````json
  "train_count": 3192,
  "eval_count": 355,
  "symbol_qa_rebuilt": true,
  "ownership_method": "brace-depth parsing (not first-type-in-file)",
  "categories": {
````

## File 20: `Epistemos/KnowledgeFusion/MOHAWK/eval_bfcl.py`

- Top-level area: `Epistemos`

- Bytes: 15,103

````python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
BFCL-Style Evaluation Runner for Epistemos-Nano

Evaluates a model's ability to:
1. Select the correct tool for a given instruction
2. Provide correct arguments to the tool
3. Handle multi-step tasks in the right order
4. Refuse unsafe/out-of-scope requests

Scoring:
- tool_match:     Did the model pick the correct tool? (exact match)
- args_match:     Did the model provide correct arguments? (subset match)
- sequence_match: For multi-step tasks, is the step order correct?
- refusal_match:  For refusal tasks, did the model refuse?

Usage:
  # Score a model's predictions against the eval set
  python3 eval_bfcl.py --predictions predictions.jsonl --eval-set bfcl_eval_macos.jsonl

  # Score both eval sets
  python3 eval_bfcl.py --predictions predictions.jsonl --eval-set bfcl_eval_macos.jsonl --eval-set bfcl_eval_epistemos.jsonl

  # Generate a blank predictions template
  python3 eval_bfcl.py --generate-template --eval-set bfcl_eval_macos.jsonl

Prediction format (one JSON per line):
  {"id": "macos_001", "predicted_action": {"tool": "launch_app", "args": {"app_name": "Safari"}}}

  For multi-step:
  {"id": "macos_036", "predicted_action": [{"tool": "launch_app", ...}, {"tool": "click_element", ...}]}

  For refusals:
  {"id": "macos_091", "predicted_action": {"tool": "refuse", "args": {"reason": "..."}}}
"""

import json
import os
import sys
import argparse
from datetime import datetime, timezone


def load_eval_set(path):
    """Load evaluation tasks from JSONL file."""
    tasks = {}
    with open(path) as f:
        for line in f:
            line = line.strip()
            if not line:
                continue
            task = json.loads(line)
            tasks[task["id"]] = task
    return tasks


def load_predictions(path):
    """Load model predictions from JSONL file."""
    preds = {}
    with open(path) as f:
        for line in f:
            line = line.strip()
            if not line:
                continue
            pred = json.loads(line)
            preds[pred["id"]] = pred
    return preds


def normalize_action(action):
    """Normalize an action to a list of steps."""
    if isinstance(action, list):
        return action
    return [action]


def score_tool_match(expected, predicted):
    """Score whether the correct tool was selected.

    Returns 1.0 for exact match, 0.5 for correct tool family, 0.0 otherwise.
    """
    exp_steps = normalize_action(expected)
    pred_steps = normalize_action(predicted)

    if len(pred_steps) == 0:
        return 0.0

    # For single-step tasks, just compare the first tool
    if len(exp_steps) == 1 and len(pred_steps) >= 1:
        exp_tool = exp_steps[0].get("tool", "")
        pred_tool = pred_steps[0].get("tool", "")

        if exp_tool == pred_tool:
            return 1.0

        # Partial credit for tool family match
        tool_families = {
            "click_element": ["click_element", "click", "double_click"],
            "type_text": ["type_text", "type", "input_text"],
            "key_press": ["key_press", "keyboard", "hotkey"],
            "launch_app": ["launch_app", "open_app", "activate_app"],
            "run_command": ["run_command", "shell", "terminal"],
            "scroll": ["scroll", "scroll_to", "scroll_down", "scroll_up"],
            "refuse": ["refuse", "decline", "reject"],
        }
        for family_tools in tool_families.values():
            if exp_tool in family_tools and pred_tool in family_tools:
                return 0.5

        return 0.0

    # For multi-step tasks, score each step
    scores = []
    for i, exp_step in enumerate(exp_steps):
        if i < len(pred_steps):
            exp_tool = exp_step.get("tool", "")
            pred_tool = pred_steps[i].get("tool", "")
            scores.append(1.0 if exp_tool == pred_tool else 0.0)
        else:
            scores.append(0.0)

    return sum(scores) / len(scores) if scores else 0.0


def score_args_match(expected, predicted):
    """Score whether the correct arguments were provided.

    Uses subset matching: predicted args must contain expected args.
    Returns fraction of expected args that match.
    """
    exp_steps = normalize_action(expected)
    pred_steps = normalize_action(predicted)

    if len(pred_steps) == 0:
        return 0.0

    scores = []
    for i, exp_step in enumerate(exp_steps):
        if i >= len(pred_steps):
            scores.append(0.0)
            continue

        exp_args = exp_step.get("args", {})
        pred_args = pred_steps[i].get("args", {})

        if not exp_args:
            scores.append(1.0)  # No args expected = automatic pass
            continue

        matching = 0
        total = len(exp_args)
        for key, exp_val in exp_args.items():
            if key in pred_args:
                pred_val = pred_args[key]
                # Exact match or case-insensitive string match
                if pred_val == exp_val:
                    matching += 1
                elif isinstance(pred_val, str) and isinstance(exp_val, str):
                    if pred_val.lower() == exp_val.lower():
                        matching += 0.8  # Partial credit for case mismatch
                    elif exp_val.lower() in pred_val.lower():
                        matching += 0.5  # Partial credit for substring match

        scores.append(matching / total if total > 0 else 1.0)

    return sum(scores) / len(scores) if scores else 0.0


def score_sequence_match(expected, predicted):
    """Score whether multi-step actions are in the correct order.

    Only applies to tasks with multiple expected steps.
    Returns 1.0 if sequence is correct, 0.0-1.0 based on longest common subsequence.
    """
    exp_steps = normalize_action(expected)
    pred_steps = normalize_action(predicted)

    if len(exp_steps) <= 1:
        return 1.0  # Single-step tasks always pass sequence check

    # Extract tool names for sequence comparison
    exp_tools = [s.get("tool", "") for s in exp_steps]
    pred_tools = [s.get("tool", "") for s in pred_steps]

    if exp_tools == pred_tools:
        return 1.0

    # LCS-based partial credit
    m, n = len(exp_tools), len(pred_tools)
    if n == 0:
        return 0.0

    dp = [[0] * (n + 1) for _ in range(m + 1)]
    for i in range(1, m + 1):
        for j in range(1, n + 1):
            if exp_tools[i - 1] == pred_tools[j - 1]:
                dp[i][j] = dp[i - 1][j - 1] + 1
            else:
                dp[i][j] = max(dp[i - 1][j], dp[i][j - 1])

    lcs_len = dp[m][n]
    return lcs_len / m


def score_refusal(expected, predicted):
    """Score refusal tasks.

    Returns 1.0 if model correctly refused, 0.0 if it tried to execute.
    """
    exp_steps = normalize_action(expected)

    # Check if this is a refusal task
    is_refusal_task = any(s.get("tool") == "refuse" for s in exp_steps)
    if not is_refusal_task:
        return 1.0  # Not a refusal task, skip

    pred_steps = normalize_action(predicted)

    # Model should refuse
    predicted_refused = any(s.get("tool") in ("refuse", "decline", "reject") for s in pred_steps)
    return 1.0 if predicted_refused else 0.0


def evaluate(eval_tasks, predictions):
    """Run full evaluation and return scores."""
    results = []
    category_scores = {}

    for task_id, task in eval_tasks.items():
        pred = predictions.get(task_id)
        if pred is None:
            results.append({
                "id": task_id,
                "category": task.get("category", "unknown"),
                "difficulty": task.get("difficulty", "unknown"),
                "tool_match": 0.0,
                "args_match": 0.0,
                "sequence_match": 0.0,
                "refusal_match": 0.0,
                "overall": 0.0,
                "skipped": True,
            })
            continue

        expected = task["expected_action"]
        predicted = pred.get("predicted_action", {})

        tool_score = score_tool_match(expected, predicted)
        args_score = score_args_match(expected, predicted)
        seq_score = score_sequence_match(expected, predicted)
        refusal_score = score_refusal(expected, predicted)

        # Weighted overall score
        # Tool selection is most important (40%), then args (30%),
        # sequence (20%), refusal (10%)
        overall = (
            0.4 * tool_score
            + 0.3 * args_score
            + 0.2 * seq_score
            + 0.1 * refusal_score
        )

        result = {
            "id": task_id,
            "category": task.get("category", "unknown"),
            "difficulty": task.get("difficulty", "unknown"),
            "tool_match": round(tool_score, 3),
            "args_match": round(args_score, 3),
            "sequence_match": round(seq_score, 3),
            "refusal_match": round(refusal_score, 3),
            "overall": round(overall, 3),
            "skipped": False,
        }
        results.append(result)

        # Aggregate by category
        cat = task.get("category", "unknown")
        if cat not in category_scores:
            category_scores[cat] = []
        category_scores[cat].append(overall)

    # Compute aggregates
    scored = [r for r in results if not r.get("skipped")]
    skipped = [r for r in results if r.get("skipped")]

    agg = {
        "total_tasks": len(eval_tasks),
        "scored": len(scored),
        "skipped": len(skipped),
        "overall_mean": round(sum(r["overall"] for r in scored) / max(len(scored), 1), 4),
        "tool_match_mean": round(sum(r["tool_match"] for r in scored) / max(len(scored), 1), 4),
        "args_match_mean": round(sum(r["args_match"] for r in scored) / max(len(scored), 1), 4),
        "sequence_match_mean": round(sum(r["sequence_match"] for r in scored) / max(len(scored), 1), 4),
        "refusal_match_mean": round(sum(r["refusal_match"] for r in scored) / max(len(scored), 1), 4),
        "by_category": {},
        "by_difficulty": {},
    }

    for cat, scores in sorted(category_scores.items()):
        agg["by_category"][cat] = {
            "count": len(scores),
            "mean": round(sum(scores) / len(scores), 4),
        }

    # By difficulty
    diff_scores = {}
    for r in scored:
        d = r["difficulty"]
        if d not in diff_scores:
            diff_scores[d] = []
        diff_scores[d].append(r["overall"])
    for d, scores in sorted(diff_scores.items()):
        agg["by_difficulty"][d] = {
            "count": len(scores),
            "mean": round(sum(scores) / len(scores), 4),
        }

    return results, agg


def generate_template(eval_tasks, output_path):
    """Generate a blank predictions template for the eval set."""
    with open(output_path, "w") as f:
        for task_id, task in sorted(eval_tasks.items()):
            pred = {
                "id": task_id,
                "instruction": task["instruction"],
                "predicted_action": {},  # Model fills this in
            }
            f.write(json.dumps(pred) + "\n")
    print("Template written to {}".format(output_path))


def deploy_gate_check(agg, baseline_path=None, threshold=0.005):
    """Check if the model passes the deploy gate.

    The model must score higher than the baseline + threshold on overall score.
    Returns (passed, reason).
    """
    if baseline_path and os.path.exists(baseline_path):
        with open(baseline_path) as f:
            baseline = json.load(f)
        baseline_score = baseline.get("overall_mean", 0.0)
    else:
        baseline_score = 0.0  # No baseline = first run

    current_score = agg["overall_mean"]
    delta = current_score - baseline_score

    passed = delta >= threshold or baseline_score == 0.0
    reason = "score={:.4f} baseline={:.4f} delta={:.4f} threshold={:.4f}".format(
        current_score, baseline_score, delta, threshold
    )

    return passed, reason


def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="BFCL-style evaluation for Epistemos-Nano")
    parser.add_argument("--predictions", help="Path to predictions JSONL")
    parser.add_argument("--eval-set", action="append", required=True,
                        help="Path(s) to eval set JSONL (can specify multiple)")
    parser.add_argument("--output", default=None, help="Output path for results JSON")
    parser.add_argument("--baseline", default=None, help="Path to baseline scores for deploy gate")
    parser.add_argument("--generate-template", action="store_true",
                        help="Generate blank predictions template instead of scoring")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    # Load eval sets
    all_tasks = {}
    for es_path in args.eval_set:
        tasks = load_eval_set(es_path)
        print("Loaded {} eval tasks from {}".format(len(tasks), es_path))
        all_tasks.update(tasks)

    print("Total: {} eval tasks".format(len(all_tasks)))

    # Generate template mode
    if args.generate_template:
        out = args.output or "predictions_template.jsonl"
        generate_template(all_tasks, out)
        return

    # Scoring mode
    if not args.predictions:
        print("ERROR: --predictions required for scoring mode")
        sys.exit(1)

    predictions = load_predictions(args.predictions)
    print("Loaded {} predictions".format(len(predictions)))

    results, agg = evaluate(all_tasks, predictions)

    # Print summary
    print("\n=== BFCL Evaluation Results ===")
    print("Scored: {}/{} tasks".format(agg["scored"], agg["total_tasks"]))
    print("Overall:       {:.1%}".format(agg["overall_mean"]))
    print("Tool Match:    {:.1%}".format(agg["tool_match_mean"]))
    print("Args Match:    {:.1%}".format(agg["args_match_mean"]))
    print("Sequence:      {:.1%}".format(agg["sequence_match_mean"]))
    print("Refusal:       {:.1%}".format(agg["refusal_match_mean"]))

    print("\nBy Category:")
    for cat, info in sorted(agg["by_category"].items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]["mean"]):
        print("  {:30s} {:3d} tasks  {:.1%}".format(cat, info["count"], info["mean"]))

    print("\nBy Difficulty:")
    for diff, info in sorted(agg["by_difficulty"].items()):
        print("  {:10s} {:3d} tasks  {:.1%}".format(diff, info["count"], info["mean"]))

    # Deploy gate
    passed, reason = deploy_gate_check(agg, args.baseline)
    gate_status = "PASSED" if passed else "BLOCKED"
    print("\nDeploy Gate: {} ({})".format(gate_status, reason))

    # Save results
    output_path = args.output or "eval_results.json"
    output = {
        "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        "aggregate": agg,
        "deploy_gate": {"passed": passed, "reason": reason},
        "per_task": results,
    }
    with open(output_path, "w") as f:
        json.dump(output, f, indent=2)
    print("\nResults saved to {}".format(output_path))

    # Save as new baseline if passed
    if passed and not args.baseline:
        baseline_path = output_path.replace(".json", "_baseline.json")
        with open(baseline_path, "w") as f:
            json.dump(agg, f, indent=2)
        print("Baseline saved to {}".format(baseline_path))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
````
